coredata.dc:title: Enhanced photocatalytic activity of Ag/CQDs/Bi2O2CO3 composite photocatalyst under full-spectrum light
coredata.dc:creator: Zhang, Zisheng
Lin, Shuanglong
Cui, Wenquan
Li, Xingang
Li, Hong
coredata.dc:description: A novel and highly efficient three-component Ag/CQDs/Bi2O2CO3 was successfully prepared. The synergistic effect of the up-conversion property of carbon quantum dots and the surface plasmon resonance from Ag effectively improves the full spectrum absorption and photogenerated charge transfer for Ag/CQDs/Bi2O2CO3 in wastewater treatment. 3-Ag/CQDs/Bi2O2CO3 can degrade methylene blue and bisphenol A by up to 93.85% and 54.88%, respectively, within 90 min under visible light. Additionally, 3-Ag/CQDs/Bi2O2CO3 has a photocatalytic activity of 73.75 and 99.25% for MB under UV light and simulated sunlight, respectively. Here, the new-styled three-component Ag/CQDs/Bi2O2CO3 may provide a valuable method to design highly efficient and full spectrum sunlight harvesting photocatalytic materials.
coredata.dcterms:subject: Semiconductors
Composite materials
Up-conversion
Photocatalytic activity

ce:caption.ce:simple-para: (a) XRD patterns and (b) UV–vis diffuse reflectance spectrum; (c) PL curve of CQDs excited by different wavelengths light (d) PL spectra of different photocatalysts.
SEM images of (a) Bi2O2CO3, (b) Ag/CQDs/Bi2O2CO3; (c) TEM and (d) HRTEM images of Ag/CQDs/Bi2O2CO3.

(a) Degradation rate constant over different light source irradiation, (b) Promotion multiple of photocatalytic processes with different samples over different lamp wavelengths. (c) Recycle experiments. (d) XRD pattern of 3-Ag/CQDs/Bi2O2CO3 before and after the photocatalytic degradation process. (e) Schematic of the possible organic wastewater degradation reaction.

head.ce:title: Enhanced photocatalytic activity of Ag/CQDs/Bi2O2CO3 composite photocatalyst under full-spectrum light

ce:affiliation.ce:label: a
ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: School of Chemical Engineering and Technology, Tianjin University, Tianjin 300072, PR China

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: School of Chemical Engineering and Technology

ce:affiliation.ce:label: b
ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: College of Chemical Engineering, North China University of Science and Technology, Tangshan 063009, PR China

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: North China University of Science and Technology

ce:affiliation.ce:label: c
ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: Department of Chemical & Biological Engineering, University of Ottawa, 161 Louis Pasteur St., Ottawa K1N6N5, Canada

ce:affiliation.ce:label: d
ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: National Engineering Research Center of Distillation Technology, Tianjin 300072, PR China

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: National Engineering Research Center of Distillation Technology

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: A novel and highly efficient three-component Ag/CQDs/Bi2O2CO3 was successfully prepared. The synergistic effect of the up-conversion property of carbon quantum dots and the surface plasmon resonance from Ag effectively improves the full spectrum absorption and photogenerated charge transfer for Ag/CQDs/Bi2O2CO3 in wastewater treatment. 3-Ag/CQDs/Bi2O2CO3 can degrade methylene blue and bisphenol A by up to 93.85% and 54.88%, respectively, within 90 min under visible light. Additionally, 3-Ag/CQDs/Bi2O2CO3 has a photocatalytic activity of 73.75 and 99.25% for MB under UV light and simulated sunlight, respectively. Here, the new-styled three-component Ag/CQDs/Bi2O2CO3 may provide a valuable method to design highly efficient and full spectrum sunlight harvesting photocatalytic materials.

ce:section.ce:para: Recently, Bi2O2CO3 has attracted the attention of many researchers. However, only ultraviolet light could be used with Bi2O2CO3 due to its wide band gap (3.2–3.4 eV) [1]. To expand the utilization range, much research has been performed, including heterostructure fabrication, metal oxide modification and metal doping [2]. Silver nanoparticles (Ag NPs) have received significant attention due to a strong surface plasmon resonance (SPR) effect. However, the biggest limitation is the absence of the utilization of longer wavelength, which is a challenge and opportunity for researchers in the future [3].

Therefore, it’s necessary to build composite photocatalytic materials to obtain efficient charge separation and full spectrum utilization. Recently, carbon quantum dots (CQDs) have been shown to demonstrate interesting physical and chemical properties, such as avirulence, excellent electron transfer capacity and an up-conversion function [4]. As we know, co-loading Ag and CQDs with 3D flower-like Bi2O2CO3 (Ag/CQDs/Bi2O2CO3) has never been attempted before. Therefore, we proposed a strategy for the simultaneous modification of Ag and CQDs onto 3D flower-like Bi2O2CO3, which may be applied for the removal of organic pollutants under full-spectrum light irradiation.

All chemicals were analytical purity and used as received. Detailed experimental and characterization methods are given in the Supporting Information. The preparation of Ag/CQDs/Bi2O2CO3 was carried out by photo-chemical reduction procedure. The obtained samples were labeled as X-Ag/CQDs/Bi2O2CO3. (X represents the mass percentage of Ag in the sample, and X = 1, 2, 3, 4, and 5 represent 1, 2, 3, 4, and 5%, respectively).

As shown in Fig. 1 a, the XRD peaks for Bi2O2CO3 can be easily found according to the standard peaks for Bi2O2CO3 (JCPDS No. 41-1488). For Ag/Bi2O2CO3 and Ag/CQDs/Bi2O2CO3, the (111) crystal plane of Ag NPs can be clearly observed at 38.1°. Nevertheless, as for CQDs/Bi2O2CO3 and Ag/CQDs/Bi2O2CO3 samples, the characteristic peaks of CQDs almost cannot be found due to their low content and high dispersion. The presence of CQDs in samples can be demonstrated by FTIR and XPS (Fig. S1). Moreover, according to the calculated results for the lattice parameters and crystal sizes (Table S1), the introduction of CQDs and Ag NPs did not change the lattice structure for Bi2O2CO3. Fig. 1b displays the DRS for different photocatalysts. Due to its wide band structure, Bi2O2CO3 has no obvious absorption capacity in the visible-light region. Surprisingly, the light absorption capacity of Ag/CQDs/Bi2O2CO3 was significantly enhanced from ultraviolet light to the NIR light regime. The synergy between CQDs and Ag was responsible for this phenomenon. The results show that Ag/CQDs/Bi2O2CO3 has a better absorption and utilization for the full-spectrum than that of Ag-AgBr/Bi2O2CO3 [1]. Fig. 1c shows the PL peaks for CQDs at different excitation wavelengths. The results demonstrate that CQDs show a significant up-conversion effect. Fig. 1d shows the photoluminescence spectra for different photocatalysts. When CQDs and Ag modify the surface of Bi2O2CO3 at the same time, the lowest peak intensity is observed. The results indicated that the synergistic effect could be more efficient to promote charge separation, and, thus, enhance the photocatalytic performance. The photoelectricity test (Fig. S2) also indicate that the synergistic effect of Ag, CQDs and Bi2O2CO3 could promote the separation and transfer of photogenic charge to the maximum extent.

Fig. 2 a shows that the flower-like Bi2O2CO3 was composed of many nanoplates with a thickness of several nanometers. Thus, more reactive sites could be provided due to the higher surface-to-volume ratio of Bi2O2CO3. Compared with Bi2O2CO3, the morphology of Ag/CQDs/Bi2O2CO3 (Fig. 2b) did not change significantly. The morphology of Ag/CQDs/Bi2O2CO3 was also further observed by TEM (Fig. 2c), which showed dense black spots evenly dispersed on the surface of the Bi2O2CO3 “flower-like” nanosheets, suggesting the existence of Ag NPs and CQDs. Additionally, in the HRTEM (Fig. 2d) of Ag/CQDs/Bi2O2CO3, the (111) crystalline of Ag could be found according to the lattice spacing (0.237 nm). Furthermore, the introduction of CQDs (002) could be demonstrated by a lattice spacing of 0.317 nm. The result indicates that Ag NPs and CQDs were successfully modified on the surface of Bi2O2CO3. Fig. S3 displays the EDX and element distribution for Ag/CQDs/Bi2O2CO3. The result strongly demonstrates the formation of Ag/CQDs/Bi2O2CO3.

Fig S4 displays that MB was a stable dye under visible light and the photocatalyst has negligible activity in dark environment. In addition, when the content of Ag NPs was 3%, the Ag/CQDs/Bi2O2CO3 has the highest photocatalytic degradation kinetic constant (0.0027 min−1). Meanwhile, to study the efficiency of the up-conversion function for CQDs and the SPR effect of Ag, the photocatalytic degradation efficiency of samples under different light sources was compared, with the results presented in Fig. 3 a. The 3-Ag/CQDs/Bi2O2CO3 exhibited an enhanced degradation rate at 1.91, 11.74 and 6.28 times higher than Bi2O2CO3 under ultraviolet, visible and simulated sunlight, respectively. The high efficiency photocatalytic performance of Ag/CQDs/Bi2O2CO3 was due to the increased light absorption capacity, increased charge amount and rapid charge transfer rate. Furthermore, Fig. 3b and Table S2 shows the photocatalytic activity of samples under different single wavelength light sources (365 nm, 450 nm, 535 nm and 630 nm). To reflect the influence of the CQDs and Ag more concretely, the promotion efficiency was calculated according to the formulae: RCQDs = (k1 − k0)/k0, RAg = (k2 − k0)/k0 and RAg/CQDs = (k3 − k0)/k0, where R is the improvement ratio, and k0, k1, k2 and k3 are the degradation rate constants for Bi2O2CO3, CQDs/Bi2O2CO3, Ag/Bi2O2CO3 and 3-Ag/CQDs/Bi2O2CO3, respectively. The results indicate that CQDs show a better up-conversion effect in the long wavelength region. In addition, the superior charge transfer properties and the SPR effect of Ag also led to enhanced photocatalytic activity. However, the synergistic effects of Ag and CQDs can best promote the photocatalytic efficiency of photocatalysts in the full-spectrum. The results show that Ag/CQDs/Bi2O2CO3 has a higher photocatalytic degradation efficiency for organic pollutants under the full-spectrum than that of others, such as CDots/WO3 [5], ZnS/CQDs [6]. Fig. S5 shows that 3-Ag/CQDs/Bi2O2CO3 has the highest photocatalytic degradation capacity (54.88%) for BPA within 90 min under visible light. In conclusion, the Ag/CQDs/Bi2O2CO3 has high photocatalytic efficiency for organic pollutants. As Fig. 3c shows, the photocatalytic efficiency of 3-Ag/CQDs/Bi2O2CO3 reaches 85.73% after five cycles of experiments, which indicates that Ag/CQDs/Bi2O2CO3 has a high photocatalytic performance and good stability. In addition, as shown in Fig. 3d, the XRD peaks for 3-Ag/CQDs/Bi2O2CO3 before and after reaction were almost the same, which indicated excellent stability for Ag/CQDs/Bi2O2CO3.

The trapping experiments and ESR measurements (Fig. S5) demonstrate that O2 −, h+ and OH are all involved in the removal of organic pollutant. According to the above results, we speculated the degradation process of organic pollutants over Ag/CQDs/Bi2O2CO3. Fig. 3e shows a tentative degradation mechanism process under simulated sunlight. Ag/CQDs/Bi2O2CO3 shows an efficient photocatalytic performance that is mainly attributed to two reasons [7,8]: (1) Enhanced spectral absorption range and conversion efficiency, (2) Rapid charge separation and transfer efficiency. As shown, ultraviolet light can be absorbed by Bi2O2CO3, following which the photoexcited electron jumped from the valence band (VB) to the conduction band (CB), leaving the hole in the VB. The holes can react with OH− or water to form ·OH or directly react with the organic molecules. Meanwhile, the e- on the CB of Bi2O2CO3 can transfer directly to the surface of CQDs and Ag NPs. The electrons received by CQDs can also combine with the holes of Ag NPs and/or transfer to Ag NPs. Then, the electrons of Ag NPs can combine with oxygen on the surface of the photocatalyst to form a superoxide oxygen radical species (O2 −) that can be used to degrade organic pollutants. Therefore, the synergistic effect of Bi2O2CO3, CQDs and Ag NPs effectively improved the absorption and utilization of full-spectrum solar light, thus, improving the photocatalytic ability.

In summary, a novel Ag/CQDs/Bi2O2CO3 prepared by a simple method shows good absorption and utilization for the full-spectrum and significant photocatalytic degradation ability. The 3-Ag/CQDs/Bi2O2CO3 presents a value of 93.85% and 54.88% for MB and BPA removal under visible-light irradiation, respectively. In addition, 3-Ag/CQDs/Bi2O2CO3 shows a photocatalytic activity of 73.75 and 99.25% for MB under UV light and simulated sunlight, respectively. Herein, it can be clearly seen that Ag/CQDs/Bi2O2CO3 has a significant photocatalytic degradation activity in the full-spectrum solar light.

ce:acknowledgment.ce:para: This work was funded by the National Natural Science Foundation of China (grant No. 21476161), the Hebei Provincial Foundation for International Cooperation (No. 15391403D) and the Natural Sciences and Engineering Research Council of Canada (Discovery). Zisheng Zhang and Shuanglong Lin both contributed equally to this work. The authors declared that they have no conflict of interest.

ce:section.ce:label: Appendix A
ce:section.ce:para: Supplementary data to this article can be found online at https://doi.org/10.1016/j.matlet.2018.09.115.

ce:section.ce:label: Appendix A
ce:section.ce:para:
The following are the Supplementary data to this article:
Supplementary data 1
